value_text: Failed daily zero calibration on both Duty and Standby MIR-FTIR instruments resulted in the unavailability of useable readings for CO, TOC, Nox, HCL, SO2 and NH3 between 12:01 and 12:39 and therefore one half hourly average and four 10-minute average readings were missing. This did not necessary mean an exceedance in the ELVs outlined in Table S3.1 as readings immediately before and after the missing periods were low and stable, and the daily averages compliant.
val_norm: failed daily zero calibration on both duty and standby mir-ftir instruments resulted in the unavailability of useable readings for co, toc, nox, hcl, so2 and nh3 between 12:01 and 12:39 and therefore one half hourly average and four 10-minute average readings were missing. this did not necessary mean an exceedance in the elvs outlined in table s3.1 as readings immediately before and after the missing periods were low and stable, and the daily averages compliant.

value_text: An elevated Dioxins and Furans reading of 0.2589ng/m3 was recorded during periodic monitoring of emissions against at permitted ELV of 0.06ng/m3. The reading was significantly higher than historical results and inconsistent with results recorded for cadmium and thallium, heavy metals and other metals tested, which remained very low.

value_text:
Please review all relevant aspects of your Environmental Management System to ensure that:
1.
You have allocated sufficient competent persons and resources to ensure compliance with all permit condition.
2.
Abating, managing and reducing emissions that you only monitor twice a year is fully embedded in your system, and that you can demonstrate that your activated carbon dosing has been optimised and that appropriate checks are in place to ensure that the dosing rate never drops below a predetermined minimum value.
3.
Legal reporting to the Environment Agency is carried out as per permit requirements and that your written procedure is robust, includes a comprehensive reporting schedule and that an appropriate training is provided to all relevant personnel.
4.
You have a system in place to demonstrate that actions listed in Compliance Assessments Reports have been closed out.

value_text:
Please carry out 2 dioxin tests per month on consecutive days over 3 months (6 in-a-row dioxin as per the Dioxins Monitoring Protocol and as required by IC3).
The Dioxin Monitoring Protocol stipulates that 6 in-a-row dioxin monitoring results are presented. This means 2 dioxin tests per month on consecutive days over 3 months. Your IC3 submission does not appear to have this information. Therefore, you are now required to carry out this monitoring activity to satisfy the requirements of IC3 and to demonstrate that your dioxin emissions are consistently below the emission limit value.

path: /content/drive/MyDrive/AQ26/incinerator annual monitoring reports/Greater Manchester & Cheshire/CP3733QJ Hooton Bio Power Annual Report 2024.xlsm
value_text:
Line 10 - ID fan VFD failure. Third party contractor undertook repairs and a courier brought new component. When conducting the repair on site the contractor found that the contactor had failed. The component is only stocked in Finland so a fast-track order was placed, and contractors returned to site on 19 June to fit the unit.
Line 20 – Low fluidization airflow has been an issue in the preceding week, so the decision was made to come offline on 15 June to clean the pre-heaters. The air preheaters were cleaned on 16 June but on restart the trommel would not move. It was subsequently found that the bearings on the drive unit had failed. Stock records showed spares on site, but these could not be located. As such a bearing was sourced locally and the rebuild of the unit commenced

path: /content/drive/MyDrive/AQ26/incinerator annual monitoring reports/QP3337AD Thalia Isle of Wight Annual Report 2024.xlsm
value_text: Schedule 5 received for a plant trip on 5th January 2024. This was caused by high temperatures in the secondary combustion chamber. Sulphur dioxide reading of 929.78mg/m3 was recorded for the period between 0830 and 0900hrs against a 30minutes ELV limit of 200mg/m3. The daily limit was also exceeded for sulphur dioxide with a daily ELV recorded as 42.52mg/m3 against a limit of 40mg/m3. These are breaches of permit condition 3.2.1.
val_norm: schedule 5 received for a plant trip on 5th january 2024. this was caused by high temperatures in the secondary combustion chamber. sulphur dioxide reading of 929.78mg/m3 was recorded for the period between 0830 and 0900hrs against a 30minutes elv limit of 200mg/m3. the daily limit was also exceeded for sulphur dioxide with a daily elv recorded as 42.52mg/m3 against a limit of 40mg/m3. these are breaches of permit condition 3.2.1.

path: /content/drive/MyDrive/AQ26/incinerator annual monitoring reports/QP3337AD Thalia Isle of Wight Annual Report 2024.xlsm
value_text: Schedule 5 received for a plant trip on 23rd January 2024. This was caused by high pressure in the primary combustion chamber. Sulphur dioxide reading of 414.88mg/m3 was recorded for the period between 1730 and 1800hrs against a 30minutes ELV limit of 200mg/m3.This is a breach of permit condition 3.2.1.
val_norm: schedule 5 received for a plant trip on 23rd january 2024. this was caused by high pressure in the primary combustion chamber. sulphur dioxide reading of 414.88mg/m3 was recorded for the period between 1730 and 1800hrs against a 30minutes elv limit of 200mg/m3.this is a breach of permit condition 3.2.1.

path: /content/drive/MyDrive/AQ26/incinerator annual monitoring reports/QP3337AD Thalia Isle of Wight Annual Report 2024.xlsm
value_text: Two Schedule 5’s were received for the 26th January 2024. This date saw multiple plant trips due to emergency shut downs on elevated combustion chamber temperatures. Sulphur dioxide readings of 613.8mg/m3 & 691.1mg/m3 was recorded for the periods between 1930hrs and 2000hrs, and 2030hrs to 2100hrs against a 30minutes ELV limit of 200mg/m3. Whilst the daily ELV was also elevated, this was within the error allowance so has not been recorded as a breach. Carbon monoxide daily ELV was recorded as 56.87mg/m3 against a daily average limit of 50mg/m3. These are breaches of permit condition 3.2.1.

path: /content/drive/MyDrive/AQ26/incinerator annual monitoring reports/QP3337AD Thalia Isle of Wight Annual Report 2024.xlsm
value_text: Schedule 5’s received for plant trips on 1st and 2nd February 2024. This was caused by high temperatures in the secondary combustion chamber. Sulphur dioxide reading of 388mg/m3 was recorded for the period between 1900 and 1930hrs on 1st February, against a 30minutes ELV limit of 200mg/m3. The daily average on the 2nd February was 91.38mg/m3 against a limit of 40mg/m3. TOC half-hourly average reading of 30.96mg/m3 against an ELV limit of 20mg/m3. These are breaches of permit condition 3.2.1.
val_norm: schedule 5’s received for plant trips on 1st and 2nd february 2024. this was caused by high temperatures in the secondary combustion chamber. sulphur dioxide reading of 388mg/m3 was recorded for the period between 1900 and 1930hrs on 1st february, against a 30minutes elv limit of 200mg/m3. the daily average on the 2nd february was 91.38mg/m3 against a limit of 40mg/m3. toc half-hourly average reading of 30.96mg/m3 against an elv limit of 20mg/m3. these are breaches of permit condition 3.2.1.

path: /content/drive/MyDrive/AQ26/incinerator annual monitoring reports/QP3337AD Thalia Isle of Wight Annual Report 2024.xlsm
value_text: Schedule 5’s received for breaches of emission limit values on 26th February 2024 due to the use of wet fuel (waste) which caused multiple blockages and fuel restrictions. Sulphur dioxide readings of 962, 552 and 266mg/m3 recorded against a 30minutes ELV limit of 200mg/m3. The daily average was 59.08mg/m3 against a limit of 40mg/m3. TOC half hourly recorded as 52 and 87mg/m3 against a limit of 20mg/m3. These are breaches of permit condition 3.2.1.
val_norm: schedule 5’s received for breaches of emission limit values on 26th february 2024 due to the use of wet fuel (waste) which caused multiple blockages and fuel restrictions. sulphur dioxide readings of 962, 552 and 266mg/m3 recorded against a 30minutes elv limit of 200mg/m3. the daily average was 59.08mg/m3 against a limit of 40mg/m3. toc half hourly recorded as 52 and 87mg/m3 against a limit of 20mg/m3. these are breaches of permit condition 3.2.1.

path: /content/drive/MyDrive/AQ26/incinerator annual monitoring reports/QP3337AD Thalia Isle of Wight Annual Report 2024.xlsm
value_text: Schedule 5 received for a plant instability on 6th and 7th March 2024. This was caused by paddle blockages and high secondary combustion chamber temperatures. Sulphur dioxide reading of 558.8mg/m3 (6th March 1430-1500hrs), 339.6mg/m3 (6th March 1630-1700hrs) and 273.6 (7th March 2130-2200hrs) were recorded a 30minute ELV limit of 200mg/m3.This is a breach of permit condition 3.2.1.
